facts.text: The Arlington BID was officially approved by the Town of Poughkeepsie Town Board in September 2001.
Arlington is the historic heart of the Town of Poughkeepsie, once bustling with shops, restaurants, banks, churches, libraries, schools, and even Vassar College.
The Arlington neighborhood was known for its comfortable mix of local businesses and residential side streets, creating a pleasant pedestrian environment.
In the 1940s and 1950s, a stock car racing facility in Arlington hosted Mutual Racing Association events, reflecting Dutchess County's post‑war auto‑racing boom.
Arlington sits at 41°41′42″N 73°53′31″W, directly west of the city of Poughkeepsie.

The Arlington Revitalization Steering Committee, formed in 2000, brought together Vassar College, the Town of Poughkeepsie, Dutchess County Economic Development Corporation, and local businesses to plan long‑term revitalization.
